questions.question: Which of the following algorithms is suitable for solving the Subset-sum problem?
questions.explanation: Backtracking and Branch-and-Bound are not typically used for the Subset-sum problem, while Approximation algorithms and Dynamic Programming can be. However, only one of the options provided is typically used for the Subset-sum problem.

questions.question: The n-Queens problem can be solved using
questions.explanation: Approximation algorithms are typically not used for the n-Queens problem. Greedy algorithms can be used for this problem, but backtracking is a common method for this problem.

questions.question: Knapsack problem can be solved using
questions.explanation: Branch-and-Bound and Approximation algorithms are not typically used for the Knapsack problem. However, both Dynamic Programming and Greedy algorithms can be used for this problem.

questions.question: Approximation algorithms are typically used for solving
questions.explanation: Approximation algorithms can be used for solving NP-complete problems, but they are not suitable for all NP problems. NP-complete problems are a subset of NP-hard problems, but not all NP problems are NP-hard or NP-complete.

questions.question: Backtracking is a type of algorithm used for solving
questions.explanation: Backtracking can be used for solving NP-complete and NP-hard problems, but it is not limited to these problems.
